learning_outcomes.description: Analyse variation in Japanese language use and the diversity of its users across social, regional, and cultural contexts.
Communicate appropriately in Japanese with diverse users by applying knowledge of linguistic variation and sociocultural norms (e.g., formality, age, region).
Collaborate with a partner or group to produce spoken and written work in Japanese.
Apply cultural knowledge and context-sensitive analysis to propose effective responses to current cross-cultural challenges.

assessments.due_date:
31/07/2026 5:00 pm

To be completed online any time before 5pm, Friday WEEK 1.

Students who are not able to meet the due date (due to late enrolment) must contact the Course Coordinator as soon as possible to ensure they comply with this requirement.
assessments.task_description:
All students who have not completed JAPN3102 at UQ are required to demonstrate that they have been correctly placed in JAPN3209. Completion of this Language Placement Questionnaire is a compliance hurdle requirement. This requirement aims to ensure that you are placed in the most appropriate language course based on your current level of linguistic proficiency. Your responses, along with the possible submission of short writing or speaking sample, help us match you to a course that aligns with your previous (formal or informal) language learning experiences.

Use of AI in this assessment

While this is an unsupervised, individual online task, it is not in your best interest to use generative AI tools. If the work you submit does not reflect your actual language ability, you may be placed in a course that is not appropriate for your level, which could negatively affect your learning and performance. This may also be considered an act of student misconduct under the UQ Student Code of Conduct due to the misrepresentation of your capabilities.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Students who are not able to meet the due date due to late enrolment, must contact the Course Coordinator as soon as possible to ensure they comply with this requirement.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description:
Worksheets are designed to support continuous engagement with course content and the progressive development of knowledge and skills throughout the semester. They provide structured opportunities for students to consolidate learning and prepare for both the final exam and the project.

The worksheets include fill-in-the-blank activities for learning key terms, exercises that develop linguistic skills, and short writing tasks that provide opportunities to apply course concepts in written Japanese. Some worksheet components are intended to be completed in conjunction with in-class activities.

Credit for these components is awarded based on students’ engagement with the associated class activities.

Use of AI in this assessment:

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Worksheets will be distributed in hard copy in Tutorial 1. Students are required to work on the worksheet in class as a part of class activities, and then take them home to review, edit and complete by the specified due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
In this assessment task, students will analyse and respond to intercultural communication scenarios presented in Japanese. Drawing on relevant course concepts and intercultural frameworks, students will identify and evaluate factors that contribute to communication challenges, consider alternative interpretations and responses, and justify contextually appropriate courses of action.

The task incorporates the critical evaluation of AI-generated content as one source of perspectives and interpretations.

Students will demonstrate their ability to communicate and justify their analyses in Japanese while reflecting on the opportunities and limitations of different approaches to intercultural communication.

The assessment is designed to develop students’ linguistic proficiency, intercultural understanding, critical thinking, and reflective judgement.

A Generative AI Use Declaration must be submitted as a part of the assessment, documenting any use of AI tools in the development of the work. A template and instructions for completing the declaration will be provided in class.

Use of AI in this assessment:

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit to Blackboard. Further instructions will be provided to students via Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
12/10/2026

Monday, WEEK 11 - In class.

This is an in-semester, School exam.
assessments.task_description:
This assessment is an in-class examination conducted during Week 11 Tutorial 1.

The examination will be completed on paper and assesses students’ understanding of the key concepts, terminology, and communication skills developed throughout Weeks 1–10 of the course. The examination may include multiple-choice questions, short-answer questions, and tasks requiring students to apply course concepts to intercultural communication scenarios. Students will be expected to demonstrate their knowledge of course content, analyse intercultural issues, and communicate their responses effectively in Japanese.

The exam will be hand-written, and no dictionaries or electronic devices are allowed.

Use of AI in this assessment:

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit the answer sheet at the end of the exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
In this assessment, students will prepare a narrated PowerPoint presentation in Japanese based on a case involving intercultural communication. Drawing on concepts and frameworks introduced in the course, students will analyse an instance of intercultural conflict, misunderstanding, or communication challenge, identify the cultural norms, values, and assumptions that contribute to the interaction, and propose culturally informed responses or solutions.

Students will demonstrate their ability to apply intercultural concepts to real-world situations, communicate complex ideas in Japanese, and justify their analyses and recommendations using evidence and course content. The presentation should integrate description, analysis, proposed solutions, and critical reflection on the broader implications of intercultural communication.

A Generative AI Use Declaration must be submitted as a part of the assessment documenting any use of AI tools in the development of the work.

Use of AI in this assessment:

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Tutorials (TUT1)

Learning the components of linguistic theory taking Japanese as the target language. Learning to apply the analytical tools used in linguistics to the analysis of Japanese. Learning the interplay between socio-cultural factors and language use within the Japanese-speaking community.
